rulings.Case Title: VENTURE STRATEGY MANAGEMENT COMPANY III, LLC, A et al VS. MATTHEW E. CRISP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Cross-Defendants' Application Pursuant To Ccp 581(F)(2) And Crc 3.1320 For Entry Of Judgment Dismissing Cross-Defendant Venture Strategy Group Llc, Dba Vsp Capital And Certain Of Defendant Matthew E. Crisp'S Previously Alleged Causes Of Action
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 23, 2007, LINE 21. CROSS DEFENDANTS' APPLICATION PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 581(F)(2) AND CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT SECTION 3.1320 FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT DISMISSING CROSS DEFENDANT VENTURE STRATEGY GROUP L.L.C., D.B.A. V.S.P. CAPITAL AND CERTAIN OF DEFENDANT MATTHEW E. CRISP'S PREVIOUSLY ALLEGED CAUSES OF ACTION. HEARING REQUIRED. THE COURT HAS DISCRETION TO GRANT THE APPLICATION BUT IT IS NOT CLEAR WHY THE COURT SHOULD DO SO. =(302/PJM/KD/AP)

rulings.Case Title: VENTURE STRATEGY MANAGEMENT COMPANY III, LLC, A et al VS. MATTHEW E. CRISP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurer To Deft Matthew E. Crisp'S Second Amended Cross-Complaint; Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Demurrer To Deft Matthew E.Crisp'S Second Amended Cross-Complaint ; Memorandum Of P/A In Support
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 23, 2007, LINE 21. PLAINTIFFS VENTURE STRATEGY MANAGEMENT COMPANY II, L.L.C., VENTURE STRATEGY MANAGEMENT COMPANY III L.L.C., AND JOHN HAMM'S Demurrer To Defendant Matthew E. Crisp'S Second Amended Cross-Complaint IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED AN EMPLOYMENT RELATIONSHIP AND DAMAGES RELATED THERETO. THE CONSPIRACY CLAIM IS BASED, AT LEAST IN PART, ON TORTIOUS CONDUCT. HEARING REQUIRED TO ADDRESS THE PROPOSED THIRD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. =(302/PJM/KD/AP)

rulings.Case Title: BRIAN P BURNS VS. THE NEIMAN MARCUS GROUP, INC., A DELAWARE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant The Neiman Marcus Group, Inc.'S Demurrer To First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 23, 2007, LINE 23. DEFENDANT THE NEIMAN MARCUS GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLARIFY THE ALLEGATIONS OF PARAGRAPHS 14 AND 15 SO AS TO DETERMINE WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAS A CLAIM UNDER COMMERCIAL CODE SECTION 3405. - THE COURT CONTINUES TO HOLD THAT THE CALIFORNIA COMMERCIAL CODE, NOT COMMON LAW, IS THE ONLY APPARENT BASIS FOR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. - THE COURT PREVIOUSLY ADDRESSED, AND DECLINED TO ORDER, PLAINTIFF TO SUE THE BANK OR BANK(S). - THE AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT SUBJECT TO DISMISSAL ON STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS GROUNDS. EACH CHECK WOULD APPEAR TO BE A SEPARATE WRONG. =(302/PJM/PM)
